Package simulation

Interface MovementStrategy

All Known Implementing Classes:
LevyWalkStrategy
public interface MovementStrategy
Strategy interface for sensor-node mobility models.

A MovementStrategy encapsulates the Markov-chain state-transition logic that governs how sensor nodes change their motion state each simulation tick. The two concrete mobility models supported by the original thesis simulator are:

  • Lévy walk — long-range ballistic motion interrupted by pauses, implemented by LevyWalkStrategy. This is the active model in the original MarkOvChain.java.
  • Brownian motion — random isotropic diffusion (commented-out transition matrix in the original MarkOvChain.java).

State semantics

The four integer states used by the simulator (and exposed as constants on MarkovChain) are:
  • 0 — IDLE: node stays in place this tick.
  • 1 — MOVE: node advances one step in its current direction.
  • 2 — CHANGE_DIR: node picks a new random direction (0–7).
  • 3 — CONTINUE: node continues in its current direction.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    nextState(int currentState, Random rng)
    Returns the next motion state given the current state.

  • Method Details

    • nextState

      int nextState(int currentState, Random rng)
      Returns the next motion state given the current state.

      The implementation samples a random value and uses the transition probability matrix to select the successor state.

      Parameters:
      currentState - current motion state (0–3)
      rng - shared random source
      Returns:
      next motion state (0–3)
